stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and usage of a major open-source LLM variant.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to theoretically model LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Addresses limitations of current monotonic scaling laws by accounting for non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining and quantization degradation.
Grounds the framework in the Shannon-Hartley theorem, offering a new perspective on model capacity and scaling.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The tags indicate its relevance within the transformer and safetensors ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies, including transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests an overview or benchmark comparison rather than a major breakthrough announcement.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a push for faster local AI inference on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tooling to the open-source community.

stories.title: Cox Media fined after bragging it spied on users through their phones
stories.author: Adi Robertson
bullet_points: Cox Media and associated marketing firms were fined by the FTC.
The controversy stemmed from claims that the companies were secretly listening to users via phones and smart devices.
The fine addresses deceptive practices regarding data collection and user privacy.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing methods to exploit the 'personalities' of AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from simpler, earlier AI hacking techniques.
The story highlights growing security risks associated with advanced AI models.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to reframe LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Addresses limitations of current monotonic scaling laws by incorporating non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining.
Uses the Shannon-Hartley theorem to unify theoretical understanding of LLM capacity and performance degradation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run Claude Code functionality with open models.
It enhances the flexibility of Ollama by bridging open-source tooling with proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Ferrari reveals its first EV, with design help from Jony Ive
stories.author: Adi Robertson
bullet_points: Ferrari has unveiled its first electric vehicle, the Luce.
The design of the Luce was a collaboration with Jony Ive and Mark Newson of LoveFrom.
This marks Ferrari's entry into the EV market and its second four-door model.

stories.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo is established as the dominant leader in the robotaxi market with a large operational fleet.
Nuro is exploring the strategic advantage of being a 'second mover' in the competitive autonomous vehicle space.
The article discusses the competitive landscape, involving rivals like Tesla, Zoox, and Motional.

stories.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The US's significant investment in quantum computing faces potential legal ambiguities.
The initiative launched the first quantum foundry company, prompting questions about its necessity.
The article suggests a critical evaluation of the current direction and needs within the quantum computing sector.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was mostly successful, but remains a work in progress.
The vehicle has not yet achieved the goal of flying to low-Earth orbit.
The report emphasizes that SpaceX still has significant hurdles to overcome with the Starship program.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to engage an ICEYE radarsat.
The capability described is noted as unusual for satellites on standard missions.
The story touches upon the increasing military and surveillance capabilities in space.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a new paradigm for agent skill development, moving beyond current hand-crafted or loosely controlled self-revision methods.
The core idea is to treat agent skills as an external state optimized using rigorous, reproducible techniques similar to weight-space optimization in deep learning.
This represents a systematic approach to controllable text-space optimization for AI agents, addressing limitations in current self-evolution methods.

stories.title: From Raw Experience to Skill Consumption: A Systematic Study of Model-Generated Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: The study focuses on how language agents improve by reusing 'skills,' which are structured procedures derived from past experience.
Domain-level and model-generated skills are highlighted as promising methods for fast, scalable adaptation within specific domains.
Despite growing extraction methods, the research notes a lack of comprehensive understanding regarding these model-generated skills.

stories.title: SPACENUM: Revisiting Spatial Numerical Understanding in VLMs
stories.author: Han Liu
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of ensuring that numerical outputs (like coordinates or action magnitudes) from Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are genuinely grounded in spatial perception.
The proposed framework, SpaceNum, aims to revisit and improve spatial numerical understanding within VLMs.
SpaceNum captures two complementary settings to analyze how numbers function in relation to spatial transitions in embodied AI environments.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck in multimodal LLMs where purely textual Chain-of-Thought struggles with fine-grained visual reasoning.
Proposes 'ETCHR,' a novel approach that decouples reasoning from image generation by using a dedicated image editing model.
Aims to overcome limitations of existing methods, which are either constrained by fixed toolkits or produce noisy intermediate images.

stories.title: Good Token Hunting: A Hitchhiker's Guide to Token Selection for Visual Geometry Transformers
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: Visual Geometry Transformers (VGTs) are powerful for 3D reconstruction but suffer from quadratic computational cost due to global attention.
The paper proposes a strategy to address scalability and efficiency issues by restricting the number of key/value tokens.
This method offers a simple yet general approach to improving the performance of VGTs in multi-view 3D tasks.

stories.title: PGT: Procedurally Generated Tasks for improving visual grounding in MLLMs
stories.author: Adriana Romero-Soriano
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in achieving fine-grained visual understanding.
It introduces Procedurally Generated Tasks (PGT), a data-driven framework to enhance visual grounding.
PGT functions both to improve model understanding and to serve as a low-cost diagnostic tool for perception failures.

stories.title: Human Decision-Making with Persuasive and Narrative LLM Explanations
stories.author: Murat Kantarcioglu
bullet_points: LLMs can enhance human decision-making in classification tasks beyond just accurate predictions.
The research focuses on the impact of generating cogent, narrative explanations from LLMs.
While prior work shows people find AI narratives trustworthy, the effect on objective decision-making is less understood.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for causal generative modeling in AI for reliable and transparent systems.
It introduces FM-CGM, a modular framework designed to integrate causal reasoning with pretrained foundation models.
The framework aims to leverage the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of large models for end-to-end visual causal reasoning.

stories.title: Complete-muE: Optimal Hyperparameter Transfer and Scaling for MoE Models
stories.author: Yan Kang
bullet_points: Introduces Complete-muE, a new framework for hyperparameter transfer in transformer models.
Specifically addresses the challenges of transferring hyperparameters between dense Feed-Forward Networks (FFN) and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) setups.
Overcomes limitations of existing tools (like $\mu$P and SDE) that cannot handle architectural changes or varying token counts in MoE scaling.

stories.title: Good Token Hunting: A Hitchhiker's Guide to Token Selection for Visual Geometry Transformers
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The paper addresses the quadratic computational cost of Visual Geometry Transformers (VGTs) used in 3D reconstruction.
The core problem is the scalability limitation caused by global attention layers in these powerful architectures.
The proposed solution is a general strategy involving restricting the number of key/value tokens processed by each query.

stories.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
stories.author: Joydeep Chandra
bullet_points: CHRONOS addresses critical failures in temporal knowledge-graph data marketplaces, specifically concerning stale indices, inaccurate pricing, and budget over-consumption.
The proposed solution is a three-layer architecture designed to unify the treatment of these challenges while maintaining explicit public and private data separation.
Key technical components include applying neural-ODE temporal decay to shortcut edges to handle evolving data.

stories.title: Training-Free Looped Transformers
stories.author: Qiang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces 'training-free looped transformers' for inference-time recurrence.
The method retrofits recurrence onto frozen, pretrained models without fine-tuning or architectural changes.
Highlights that naive block reapplication degrades performance, emphasizing the importance of the loop application strategy.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for causal generative modeling to build reliable and transparent AI systems capable of counterfactual reasoning.
The proposed FM-CGM framework unifies causal constraints with the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of pretrained foundation models.
FM-CGM is presented as a modular framework specifically designed for end-to-end visual causal reasoning.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: The research challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a 'strong' teacher model is necessary for effective student training.
The study investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong) in LLM pretraining.
Findings suggest that proper mixing during language modeling can allow for successful distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger than the student.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a new method for developing agent skills, moving beyond current hand-crafted or loosely controlled self-revision techniques.
The core idea is to treat the agent's skill as an external state optimized using rigorous, reproducible methods similar to weight-space optimization.
This represents a systematic approach to controllable text-space optimization for AI agents, aiming for reliable improvement under feedback.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of purely textual Chain-of-Thought reasoning in Multimodal LLMs for complex visual tasks.
It proposes a novel approach, ETCHR, that decouples reasoning from image generation by using a dedicated image editing model.
This method aims to overcome the constraints of fixed toolkits and noisy intermediate images found in current 'think with images' paradigms.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: Challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a stronger teacher model is necessary for effective student training.
Investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong) by manipulating model sizes and training budgets.
Suggests that proper mixing during language modeling can allow for successful distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger than the student.

stories.title: Hierarchical Concept Geometry in Language Models Emerges from Word Co-occurrence
stories.author: Matthieu Wyart
bullet_points: Proposes a distributional theory explaining how hypernymy ('is-a' relations) is encoded geometrically in language models.
The research links word co-occurrence patterns (based on WordNet) to the geometric structure of word embeddings.
The study provides a theoretical characterization of the embedding Gram matrix spectrum under specific co-occurrence kernel conditions.

stories.title: ChartFI: Benchmarking Faithfulness and Insightfulness of Chart Descriptions from Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Siming Chen
bullet_points: The paper introduces ChartFI, a new benchmark to evaluate the faithfulness and insightfulness of chart descriptions generated by Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Current benchmarks are criticized for using simple, homogeneous charts and relying on shallow, fact-enumeration descriptions.
Accurate chart descriptions are crucial for accessibility, cross-modal retrieval, and enabling users to extract insights from complex visualizations.

stories.title: Show HN: Write your BPF programs in Go, not C
stories.author: boratanrikulu
bullet_points: A new tool/approach allows BPF programs to be written in Go instead of traditional C.
This shift aims to improve developer experience and potentially simplify BPF programming.
The announcement is a 'Show HN,' indicating a community-driven project showcase.

stories.title: An npm Package for AI Agent Orchestration Just Shipped With Its Front Door Unlocked. Here's What the CVE Actually Reveals.
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: A new npm package for AI Agent Orchestration has been released.
The package has a security vulnerability (CVE) that has been identified.
This highlights the growing security scrutiny within the rapidly expanding MCP ecosystem.

stories.title: How to Fix Tool-Use Loops in Autonomous Coding Agents
stories.author: alanwest
bullet_points: The article addresses a common failure mode in autonomous coding agents: tool-use loops.
It explains the underlying reasons why these agents become stuck in repetitive tool usage.
The content provides four specific, actionable solutions to prevent these loops.
